evidence.template: Support your argument{topic_hint} with concrete facts, data, or widely recognised evidence. Cite the source or institution behind each claim. Avoid invented statistics.

socratic.intro: Challenge opponent's assumptions{target} with probing questions.
question_templates: What are the consequences if you are wrong?

citation.instruction: Name your source for every factual claim you make.
citation.challenge_template: After your main argument, note that the opponent's claim ('{snippet}...') requires a verifiable source.

tone_moderation.instruction: Be direct and assertive. Avoid aggressive language. No personal attacks.

rebuttal.attack_template: Attack this specific claim: "{strongest_claim}". Refute it directly with evidence or logic. Then introduce ONE new argument angle not yet raised in this debate.

angles: a specific counterexample that undermines the opponent
impact on marginalized or vulnerable groups
comparative analysis with similar cases or countries
progression.template: Round {round_num}: You MUST introduce a FRESH perspective. Explore the **{angle}** of this debate — an angle not yet fully addressed. Do not recycle previous arguments.
